sound_type: piping wheep-wheep -- piercing, loud, repeated piping call that cuts through surf noise; high-pitched, insistent; E=5
ecosystem: Rocky shores -- intertidal zone, rocky headlands, jetties, offshore rocks, mussel beds. Exclusive to rocky marine shores. Never inland, never sandy beaches.
season: Year-round resident. Permanent territories on specific stretches of rocky shore. Piping year-round, peak during breeding (spring/summer).

coordinates.description: Black Oystercatcher on rocky shores of the San Juan Islands, Olympic Peninsula outer coast, and rocky headlands of Puget Sound.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST HAEMATOPODIDAE entry. Oystercatcher family enters the engine. Rocky shore specialist. Red-orange bill adapted for shellfish. Indicator species for intertidal health. ~10,000 coastwide.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Haematopus from Greek 'haima' (blood) + 'pous' (foot) -- blood-foot, referring to red legs. bachmani honoring John Bachman, collaborator with Audubon.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Haematopus -- 12 species worldwide. All coastal shorebirds with long red-orange bills.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. First Charadriiformes entry in recent sequence. Shorebird order enters.
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Haematopodidae. The rocky shore sentinel. E=5. Narrowest habitat band in the engine.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Piping: 3000-5000 Hz. High-pitched, piercing. Designed to cut through low-frequency surf noise.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 3500-4500 Hz. The piercing core of the wheep call.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual notes: 0.2-0.4 seconds. Sequences: 3-20+ notes. Territorial disputes can last minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: WHEEP! WHEEP! WHEEP! -- piercing, insistent, repeated whistles. Accelerates during alarm. Piping displays between territorial neighbors.
song_metrics.note_types: Wheep piping (primary -- territorial/alarm/flight), contact (softer piping -- pair/family), alarm (accelerated, higher-pitched piping).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated single notes in rapid sequence. Often given in flight. Piping displays: synchronized piping between pair members walking along territory boundary.
song_metrics.timbre: Piercing, whistled, clean. Cuts through ambient surf noise. Designed for the high-noise environment of rocky shores.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Loud. Dominates local soundscape of rocky headlands. Carries well above surf and wind noise.
song_metrics.tempo: Rapid repetition. 2-4 notes per second during alarm. Slower during routine territorial calling.
song_metrics.context: Territorial defense. Pair bonding. Flight call. Alarm. Piping displays between neighbors. Most behavioral contexts.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal across Pacific coast range. Consistent piping character from Alaska to Baja.
song_metrics.key_property: WHEEP-WHEEP PIPING -- piercing, high-pitched, surf-cutting. FIRST HAEMATOPODIDAE. Red-orange bill. Rocky shore specialist. Intertidal indicator. E=5. v1.49.306.

plumage.overall: Entirely black. Bright red-orange bill. Pink legs. Yellow eye with red eye-ring. No seasonal variation. Dark plumage against dark rock.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: All black body with vivid red-orange bill. Unmistakable on rocky shores.
plumage.seasonal_variation: None. Black plumage and red-orange bill year-round.
plumage.flight_pattern: Low over water along shoreline. Strong, direct wingbeats. Often piping in flight.

physical_description.overall: Length: 42-47 cm. Weight: 500-700g. Stocky shorebird. All black with vivid red-orange bill.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Black body + red-orange bill + pink legs. On rocky shores only.
physical_description.field_id: Stocky black shorebird on rocky coast. Bright red-orange bill unmistakable. Listen for loud piercing WHEEP! WHEEP! piping.

nodes.title: Piercing Piping -- Surf-Cutting Call
nodes.description: WHEEP! WHEEP! WHEEP! 3000-5000 Hz. Piercing, high-pitched, designed to carry above surf and wind noise. E=5. Territorial, alarm, flight. Piping displays between neighbors.

nodes.title: Red-Orange Bill -- Precision Shellfish Tool
nodes.description: 6-8 cm laterally compressed blade. Three techniques: stabbing (open bivalves), hammering (closed shells), probing (crevice invertebrates). Learned from parents over months. Most specialized feeding tool in the engine.

nodes.title: Intertidal Indicator Species
nodes.description: Presence = healthy rocky shore. Permanent territories. Sensitive to disturbance. 8,900-11,000 coastwide. Conservation rapid assessment tool. Habitat fidelity absolute.

nodes.title: Rocky Shore Specialist
nodes.description: Exclusive to rocky intertidal. Never inland. Never sandy beaches. Territories of 100-500m held year-round for decades. The narrowest habitat band of any species in the engine.

nodes.title: First Haematopodidae -- Oystercatcher Family Enters
nodes.description: Haematopodidae enters at degree 166. 12 species worldwide. All coastal. All with red-orange bills. Black Oystercatcher is the Pacific coast specialist. First Charadriiformes in recent sequence.

cross_references.relationship: HABITAT CONTRAST! Crane: open field, 2-4 km carry, E=6. Oystercatcher: rocky shore, surf-cutting piping, E=5. Open landscape vs narrow coastal strip.
BILL CONTRAST! Turkey: short omnivore bill. Oystercatcher: long specialized shellfish bill. Both loud. Different feeding ecology.
COASTAL EDGE! Black Swift on coastal cliffs. Oystercatcher on rocky shores below. Both tied to the coastal edge.
SPECIALIST VS GENERALIST! Coot: freshwater generalist. Oystercatcher: rocky shore specialist. Ubiquitous vs rare and localized.
